meta.title: Greenways site knowledge cards
meta.description: Grounded claims agents may cite in chat — numbers must match linked JSON or official pages. Additive only; never replaces existing answers.

cards.proseTemplate: For example, our **{title}** on Greenways shows running cost around **€{baselineMonthlyEur}/mo → €{proposedMonthlyEur}/mo**, capex **~€{capexEur}**, with an illustrative **€{grantEur}** grant on the chart — open the projection module for payback timing.

Our **{title}** scenario models **€{baselineMonthlyEur}/mo → €{proposedMonthlyEur}/mo** with capex **~€{capexEur}** and an example **€{grantEur}** grant — useful when warewash load drives peak water and heat.

On the **wok line** worked example, Greenways shows **€{baselineMonthlyEur}/mo → €{proposedMonthlyEur}/mo** gas-equivalent duty with **~€{capexEur}** capex and **€{grantEur}** illustrative grant — {savingsNote}.
Cold chain upgrades often start with numbers: our **{title}** chart uses **€{baselineMonthlyEur}/mo → €{proposedMonthlyEur}/mo**, **~€{capexEur}** capex, **€{grantEur}** example grant.

Kitchen **HVAC heat recovery** on our projection page illustrates **€{baselineMonthlyEur}/mo → €{proposedMonthlyEur}/mo** with **~€{capexEur}** capex and **€{grantEur}** example grant — fan and gas heating offset combined.

cards.prose: On Greenways, **etl_*** rows mirror the UK **Energy Technology List** — products are independently verified and typically in the **top 25%** of their class, so procurement risk is lower than brochure claims alone. Open the official ETL module to read the scheme in DESNZ's own words.

A practical ETL path on Greenways: **official Energy Technology List** → **ETL finder** shortlist → **equipment deep dive** side-by-side → **savings projection** for payback → **Andrieus** for grants and **Vincent** for finance.

Before heavy kitchen capex, **measuring use** often pays back fast: published office **aM&T** programmes commonly find **4–20%** savings (**~10–15%** typical), and tenant sub-metering in housing often lands **10–15%** when bills reflect real use — our monitoring guide walks through methods and European case studies.

Fabric first: lowering heating and cooling baseload with **insulation** and ventilation discipline often makes cookline upgrades smaller and payback faster — our renovation and insulation modules order fabric, HVAC, and equipment swaps sensibly.

Typical finance stack on Greenways: model payback in **savings projection**, check non-repayable support in the **finance finder grants** tab or **Andrieus**, then match **BNPL**, **equipment finance**, or **green loans** to your capex band — tariffs and ticker context explain why efficient kit matters when €/kWh moves.

cards.proseTemplate: Our live feed lists **{title}** — *{line}* — as the EU-wide entry for postcode tariff compares; standing charge and unit rate both matter before you switch.
For **NL hospitality**, the feed highlights **{title}** — *{line}* — pair VAT-aware estimates with **Vincent** for payback when cookline upgrades follow a renewal.
UK renewals: **{title}** (*{line}*) is the feed row for renewable-backed supply — confirm backing and renewal dates on the supplier page, not just the headline green label.

cards.prose: The **deals ticker hub** runs three lanes — **energy**, **water**, and **sustainability** — plus search; feed rows are curated entry points, so always confirm price and contract terms on the linked portal.

cards.proseTemplate: Water savings often start upstream: **{title}** (*{line}*) links products, tips, grants, and calculator — good before heavy kitchen retrofit spend.

cards.prose: Zara's rule on Greenways: the best offer often cuts **lifetime cost**, not headline price — match tariff, grant, rebate, or equipment incentive to real usage; misleading free or limited-time claims get flagged.

cards.proseTemplate: Greenways tracks **{schemeCount}** active funding schemes (**{nlCount}** NL · **{ukCount}** UK) — set your region filter, then open the restaurant or EU portal to compare before you apply.
For Dutch discovery, **{title}** is the official starting point — *{descriptionSnippet}* — use the government finder before shortlisting rows from our catalogue.
NL equipment capex often stacks **MIA & Vamil** — *{descriptionSnippet}* — verify asset lists and Belastingdienst rules on the official page.
UK pathways include **{title}** — *{descriptionSnippet}* — delivery varies by linked programme; confirm eligibility on gov.uk.

cards.prose: On the marketplace, grants attach **per product** after scheme enrichment — browse an ETL-listed item or equipment deep dive to see matched tax relief and subsidies for your region before you shortlist schemes in chat.

When a scheme row shows a deadline in our catalogue, treat it as a **guide only** — programmes pause, extend, or close without notice; always confirm open status on the official link before planning around a date.

cards.proseTemplate: Zyanne searches **three utility lanes** — **{waterCount}** water · **{electricityCount}** electricity · **{gasCount}** gas rows in our sustainable catalog ({catalogCount} total), plus marketplace listings with grant overlays.

cards.prose: Two columns on Greenways: **On Greenways** marketplace products (verified listings with grants) and **Market alternative** catalog rows for gas, water, and retrofit options not yet on the marketplace — chat is fast search; open finders for full side-by-side compare.

**Greenways Marketplace** (greenwaysmarket.com) is the shop — green electronics, restaurant equipment, and departments. **Greenways tools** on Render are compare, grants, deep dive, and finders. Chat answers from catalog data; open the About or shop hub module for the marketplace mission and browse links.
Water lane path: read the **Water Saving Guide** for fixtures, leaks, and dishwasher priorities, then open **Water Saving Finder** to compare products and stack grants with **Andrieus**.

cards.proseTemplate: Warewash example: **{name}** benchmarks **{dailyWaterLitres} L/day** water duty in our catalog — use the guide + finder before swapping hood or undercounter dishwashers.
Cold storage example: **{name}** models **{dailyKwh} kWh/day** in our catalog — *{summarySnippet}* — pair with equipment deep dive for ETL marketplace alternatives.
Gas cookline: **{name}** cites **{gasReductionPct}** illustrative gas savings in our catalog — stack with Artemis deep dive and savings projection before full burner replacement.
Cheryce's latest **{editionTitle}** edition carries **{storyCount}** curated headlines — lead story: **{leadStoryTitle}** — open the sustainability newsletter module for the full roundup.
Today's briefing pulls **{storyCount}** headlines from **{edition}** — including **{leadStoryTitle}** — pair with the energy ticker when wholesale moves change upgrade timing.
June policy watch: **{leadStoryTitle}** — *{leadStorySummary}* — labelling and EPREL simplification affect how you compare efficient equipment before capex.
Importer runway: **{leadStoryTitle}** — *{leadStorySummary}* — Q3 is the window to validate embedded emissions before the annual declaration.
**New in Tech ({edition})**: **{leadStoryTitle}** — *{leadStorySummary}* — engineering teams should map EPREL and QR registry work before Q3 compliance.

The **sustainability map** holds **{caseStudyCount}** case studies with savings stats plus **{directoryCount}** directory organisations — companies and networks that can **inspire your sustainability journey**. For **product efficiency benchmarks**, use the **Product Finder**.

cards.prose: Cheryce curates **sustainability** and **New in Tech** monthly editions from content-ops — chat gives impact lines and headline picks; full newsletters and reference links open in module tablets on the right.

Edwardo's rule on Greenways: **measure before you buy** — whole-building bills hide cookline, refrigeration, and overnight HVAC waste; submetering and the monitoring guide often find **4–20%** savings before capex.

cards.proseTemplate: Greenways dashboard cost model: **{tariffSummary}** — e.g. {formulaExample} — baseline vs actual KPI chips use the same tariff floor across portfolio and utility detail.
Restaurant sensor rollout: Edwardo prioritises **{priorityCount}** signal types — start with **{topSensor}** — demo sites include **{exampleSites}** in our portfolio data.

cards.prose: The **sensor intelligence dashboard** shows demo power, gas, water, and equipment lanes across a multi-site restaurant portfolio — use it to see what Edwardo monitors once hardware is live, then open utility detail for KPI context.

cards.proseTemplate: Edwardo's **Verify selected** sidebar runs **{checkCount}** read-only freshness checks (grants, products, catalog, deals, news, agents) — it **does not** run integrator or build scripts on your server.

News knowledge check: **{storyCount}** policy/funding KB stories (updated **{updatedAt}**) plus Cheryce's latest **{editionTitle}** monthly edition — Edwardo reads files on disk; ask **Cheryce** for headline impact lines.
Deals feed verify: **{dealCount}** spotlight rows · last built **{generatedAt}** — Edwardo only reads this file; **Zara** owns deal browsing and the ticker hub.

cards.prose: Equipment **deep dive** adds a systems view — product-level kWh, gas, and water per line plus decision matrix vs ETL alternatives; pair meter data from Edwardo with Artemis specs before you finance with Vincent.

For restaurants, **Scope 3** is usually the largest emissions bucket — ingredients, packaging, transport, waste, and delivery before gas and electricity in the building. Credible plans start with menu design, supplier choices, and waste prevention.

**Net zero** means deep cuts first — offsets only for unavoidable remainder. For hospitality, that usually means Scope 3 menu and waste work plus efficient kit and renewable supply, not buying credits instead of changing operations.

**CSRD** is the EU corporate sustainability reporting rule — larger groups and their value chains must disclose environmental, social, and governance impacts. Smaller operators may feel it through big-brand supplier questionnaires.
**Circular economy** in kitchens means designing out waste — reuse, repair, returnable packaging, and organics collection — not only recycling what already left the pass.

**Energy efficiency** is doing the same work with less kWh and gas — ETL-listed kit, insulation, smart HVAC, and submetering on cookline and cold rooms. It cuts bills when tariffs move, not only carbon on paper.
**Food waste** doubles the pain — emissions from growing food that is never eaten, plus disposal. Forecasting, station-level tracking, and portion discipline often beat another efficiency brochure.
**CO₂e** lets you compare methane, nitrous oxide, and CO₂ on one scale — menu carbon labels and supplier reports usually speak in grams CO₂e per dish or per kg ingredient.
**ESG** bundles environmental, social, and governance criteria investors and large buyers use — emissions, labour, food safety, and board oversight matter alongside your menu story.
